title: Fundraiser

jokes.text: The inter-nerds are on it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of online campaign for Cornstarch Utensils
jokes.punchline: Combining 'internet' and 'nerds' into awkward portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Efficient wordplay that reveals Mike's age/disconnect from modern technology. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, is that a poppy seed there in your teeth?
jokes.setup: Mike advising Selina to smile for senators meeting
jokes.punchline: Immediately pointing out embarrassing dental issue
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe humor - undermining advice with immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: well, then the VEEP has landed.
jokes.setup: Selina discussing getting Cornstarch Utensils in federal buildings
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'the Eagle has landed' phrase with her title
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Selina's ego and delusions of grandeur about minor policy wins.

jokes.text: Like a big, beautiful eagle.
jokes.setup: Amy saying they're working on 'landing' Selina
jokes.punchline: Extending the metaphor to absurd conclusion
jokes.explanation: Amy's dry extension of Selina's metaphor shows her simultaneous support and skepticism.

jokes.text: He has so many tubes in him, he looks like a set of bagpipes.
jokes.setup: Asked if Senator Reeves is dead yet, Mike says he's mostly intravenous
jokes.punchline: Vivid, absurd comparison of dying senator to musical instrument
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with a perfectly specific visual metaphor. Shows Mike's callousness while being genuinely funny.

jokes.text: It's like a wheelchair for the eye.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether glasses make her look weak
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific metaphor for glasses
jokes.explanation: Absurd metaphor that reveals Selina's vanity and weird way of thinking. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: I'm not a sniper.
jokes.setup: Asked 'Is this the right room? Are we early?' regarding Senator Dorsey's 2:00 appointment
jokes.punchline: Random defensive statement about not being a sniper
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist comment that's mildly funny for its unexpectedness but lacks clear logic.

jokes.text: There aren't enough people to fill a fucking canoe in here.
jokes.setup: Amy asks what's going on after Selina's bad 'okay'
jokes.punchline: Colorful description of poor turnout at their event
jokes.explanation: Vivid metaphor for poor attendance. Character-appropriate vulgarity and frustration.

jokes.text: Is that a map joke?
jokes.setup: Mike introduces Senator Dudley who's 'interested in maps'
jokes.punchline: Selina awkwardly asking if this is meant to be funny
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about whether something is supposed to be funny. Shows Selina's confusion.

jokes.text: I am in a room with three people and a fuckload of quiche.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining the disaster of her senate meeting to Mike
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment combining profanity with mundane detail
jokes.explanation: Efficient comedy contrasting high political stakes with embarrassing reality. Classic Veep formula.

jokes.text: Do these not bend the fuck back? What am I supposed to do with this? I'm supposed to eat around corners?
jokes.setup: Selina struggling with inflexible Cornstarch utensils
jokes.punchline: Escalating frustration with absurd eating scenario
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalating into absurd scenarios. Shows the impracticality of her own initiative.

jokes.text: How do you suggest that I mingle with this few people? Did Simon mingle with Garfunkel?
jokes.setup: Amy suggesting Selina should mingle at the poorly attended event
jokes.punchline: Comparing small gathering to musical duo
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that perfectly captures the absurdity of 'mingling' with so few people.

jokes.text: Well, you know what I say, it's a dirty job and I love to do it.
jokes.setup: Dan praising the clean jobs initiative
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'dirty job but someone's got to do it'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that shows Selina trying too hard to be clever and failing.

jokes.text: Power. I'm kidding!
jokes.setup: Barbara asking what she's been missing
jokes.punchline: Truthful answer immediately retracted as joke
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about the VP's lack of power, with awkward backpedaling.

jokes.text: She's middle of the road. She's mediocre, really. Of all the -ocres, she's the mediest.
jokes.setup: Asked about working for Senator Hallowes
jokes.punchline: Escalating criticism ending in made-up superlative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay with invented superlative. Shows Dan's wit and disloyalty perfectly.

jokes.text: All right, look, watch me, autismo. You take the little pod, put the little pod in the fucking hole, shut the fucking lid, hit the fucking button for two fucking seconds.
jokes.setup: Gary struggling with coffee machine
jokes.punchline: Aggressive, profanity-laden explanation with ableist slur
jokes.explanation: Crude humor relying on excessive profanity and offensive language. Low craft despite some escalation.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, next time pack an espresso machine in your big fuckin' bitch bag.
jokes.setup: Gary saying the coffee machine operation was too quick to follow
jokes.punchline: Calling Gary's bag 'The Leviathan' a 'bitch bag'
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier bag reference with crude escalation. Moderately effective character comedy.

jokes.text: And you do not want to fuck with those guys because they fuck in a very unpleasant fashion.
jokes.setup: Warning about pissing off oil companies
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor using 'fuck' in different contexts
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation using wordplay. Character-appropriate tough talk from Barbara.

jokes.text: I could draw you a diagram, but it would be a very simple diagram.
jokes.setup: Explaining the simple relationship between plastics and oil funding
jokes.punchline: Condescending offer to draw obvious diagram
jokes.explanation: Deadpan putdown implying Selina's stupidity. Effective condescension.

jokes.text: Oh, I can handle geometry, Barbara, all right?
jokes.setup: Barbara's condescending diagram comment
jokes.punchline: Defensive response missing that it would be a simple organizational chart, not geometry
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's defensiveness and slight misunderstanding of the situation.

jokes.text: Rapey Reeves.
jokes.setup: Hearing that Senator Reeves just died
jokes.punchline: Immediate unflattering nickname for the deceased
jokes.explanation: Economical dark humor revealing the senator's reputation. Shows Washington cynicism.

jokes.text: He was full of bourbon. And he grabbed my left tit.
jokes.setup: Barbara asking if Reeves was full of wisdom
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was drunk and sexually inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation undermining the 'wisdom' expectation. Specific detail makes it funnier and more real.

jokes.text: Because that guy is a weapons-grade retard. I think you might have been hoist by your own retard there.
jokes.setup: Asking if Selina fired her tweet writer
jokes.punchline: Military metaphor combined with variation of 'hoist by your own petard'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on classic phrase, though using offensive language. Shows Barbara's wit.

jokes.text: Yeah, it was like a funeral for a homeless guy.
jokes.setup: Mike apologizing about the Senate meeting
jokes.punchline: Comparing poor attendance to imagined sparsely attended funeral
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, economical metaphor that's both dark and specific. Perfect encapsulation of the event's failure.

jokes.text: 'Selina, hi. I'm melting. I'm melting.'
jokes.setup: Amy asking how the meeting with Hallowes went
jokes.punchline: Imitating Hallowes as the Wicked Witch of the West
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference showing Selina's disdain. Moderately effective character moment.

jokes.text: You know my motto, 'I don't want to know.' Anyways, what motto? I don't have a motto.
jokes.setup: Selina claiming plausible deniability about press strategy
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself about having a motto
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's scattered thinking and self-contradiction.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, what was I just gonna say? Oh. I don't know. It just went out of my head.
jokes.setup: Gary bringing her coffee
jokes.punchline: Selina completely losing her train of thought mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Realistic but minor moment of scatterbrained behavior. Relatable cringe.

jokes.text: Close the door. On your way out, close it. Quickly. Quick, quick. Quick, quick. God Almighty.
jokes.setup: Jonah arriving from the White House
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic attempts to get rid of him
jokes.explanation: Escalating desperation showing everyone's disdain for Jonah. Good character work.

jokes.text: I love Italian, Jonah, but still, unfortunately, really dislike you.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking if Amy likes Italian food
jokes.punchline: Polite rejection that becomes brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure and economy. Amy's directness contrasted with polite phrasing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, it's the guy from 'My Left Foot.'
jokes.setup: Jonah entering the room
jokes.punchline: Comparing tall, awkward Jonah to disabled character from film
jokes.explanation: Cruel reference humor that's economical but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: 'Cause you need to put it up on that star chart on your refrigerator.
jokes.setup: Jonah bragging about talking to the president four times
jokes.punchline: Treating his bragging like a child's achievement chart
jokes.explanation: Effective condescension treating Jonah like a child. Good character consistency for Amy.

jokes.text: And in each of those did he say, 'Someone get this freak the fuck away from me'?
jokes.setup: Jonah listing his four interactions with the president
jokes.punchline: Imagining the president's actual reaction to Jonah
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that undermines Jonah's bragging with realistic presidential reaction.

jokes.text: Obviously they won't ask you guys because you have no... um, you just have other areas of expertise. Yeah, clean jobs, Yemen, mission to Mars.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the VP won't get hard questions at fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting them, then listing increasingly absurd 'expertise' areas
jokes.explanation: Jonah's social incompetence leading to escalating insults. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Uh, don't tell me what to do, Doogie Fucking Howser.
jokes.setup: Jonah telling Mike he needs to be at the fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Comparing young-looking Jonah to teenage TV doctor
jokes.explanation: Standard reference humor showing Mike's disdain. Moderately effective but not original.

jokes.text: That address makes me hard.
jokes.setup: Saying he's going back to the White House
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual reference about his workplace
jokes.explanation: Cheap sexual humor that's character-appropriate for clueless Jonah but not clever.

jokes.text: You looked tired a lot and the hat.
jokes.setup: Selina asking about her two biggest campaign mistakes
jokes.punchline: Blunt, unflattering assessment focusing on superficial issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike response - brutally honest, unflattering, and focused on superficial rather than substantive issues.

jokes.text: Oh, Dan is a shit. You want to expand on that? Sure. He's a massive and total shit.
jokes.setup: Selina asking what Mike thinks of Dan
jokes.punchline: Escalating from simple assessment to emphatic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Mike's characteristic bluntness. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: When you first meet him, you think surely to God this man can't be as big a shit as he seems, but he is. 'Cause like if there were a book with covers made of shit, you'd think, 'That's intriguing. I wonder what's in this book that they saw fit to give it covers made of pure shit.' And then you open it and... shit.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Dan is a shit
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor about a book made of shit that contains only shit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor that perfectly captures disappointment in Dan. Creative, character-specific, and quotable.

jokes.text: She's got a small mustache. It's a little disturbing. Don't stare at it, okay?
jokes.setup: Pointing out Congresswoman Clements to Selina
jokes.punchline: Immediately drawing attention to embarrassing physical feature
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor about awkward social situation. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You could use celery as a kind of shovel.
jokes.setup: Discussing politicized utensil situation at fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Suggesting improvised eating implement
jokes.explanation: Absurd but practical solution showing Gary's helpfulness in ridiculous situations.

jokes.text: She's fun, she's sexy. She can advance my career. I really like her.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's dating his boss's daughter
jokes.punchline: Listing career advancement among romantic qualities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment showing his calculating nature mixed with self-deception about romance.

jokes.text: I'll redact your fucking face.
jokes.setup: Being told to redact parts of the VP's speech
jokes.punchline: Threatening to redact Dan's face
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of bureaucratic language into personal threat. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: This has been pencil-fucked completely? Uh, yes, front and back. Very little romance.
jokes.setup: Asking about edits to her speech
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor for extensive editing
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective metaphor for heavy editing. Amy's follow-up enhances the joke.

jokes.text: What's left here? I've got 'hello' and I have... Prepositions.
jokes.setup: Looking at her heavily edited speech
jokes.punchline: Only greeting and grammar words remain
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the absurdity of the censorship. Specific detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's your 'Sergeant Pepper's.'
jokes.setup: Telling Selina that Senate vote reform is a home run topic
jokes.punchline: Comparing boring political topic to landmark album
jokes.explanation: Absurd comparison between policy topic and classic music. Shows Amy's desperation.

jokes.text: Who knew he wore kitten heels?
jokes.setup: Saying she's stepped into the president's shoes
jokes.punchline: Literalizing the metaphor with feminine footwear
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay literalizing common metaphor. Gets a polite chuckle but not exceptional.

jokes.text: He's more of a stilettos kind of guy.
jokes.setup: Clarifying the president doesn't actually wear kitten heels
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with even more feminine footwear
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation that doesn't improve on the original joke. Shows Selina trying too hard.

jokes.text: He died. He's... he's dead now.
jokes.setup: Trying to eulogize Senator Reeves in her speech
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious about his death awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Awkward public speaking moment showing Selina's discomfort. Realistic cringe.

jokes.text: Politics is about people. It's about people.
jokes.setup: Selina struggling through her heavily edited speech
jokes.punchline: Repeating vague platitude when she has nothing to say
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment of public speaking failure. Shows desperation when content is stripped away.

jokes.text: That's because Mike McClintock there threw him into the Potomac River.
jokes.setup: Explaining that their tweet writer can't afford his iPhone contract
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Mike literally murdered him
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from firing to murder. Good escalation but not exceptional.

jokes.text: we were hoist by our own retard.
jokes.setup: Explaining the Cornstarch tweet problem
jokes.punchline: Callback to Barbara's earlier phrase about being 'hoist by your own retard'
jokes.explanation: Callback using offensive language. Sets up the controversy but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: What if Tom Hanks dies? I'm not wishing that. I'm saying anything could happen... Tom Hanks could die, there could be a forest fire in L.A.
jokes.setup: Trying to suggest the 'retard' story won't be in papers
jokes.punchline: Bizarre hope for celebrity death or natural disaster to overshadow scandal
jokes.explanation: Absurd crisis management strategy showing Mike's desperation. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: How did you get this job? You really want to base your press strategy based on trying to figure out when Tom Hanks is gonna...
jokes.setup: Mike's Tom Hanks death strategy
jokes.punchline: Amy's incredulous reaction to the absurd plan
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to Mike's absurdity. Amy's exasperation is relatable.

jokes.text: Making physics fun.
jokes.setup: Gary explaining static electricity and dirt retention
jokes.punchline: Dry comment on his scientific explanation
jokes.explanation: Economical deadpan response to Gary's over-explanation. Mild but effective.

jokes.text: Every minute that we delay, 'Retard' goes up a font size.
jokes.setup: Discussing urgency of damage control
jokes.punchline: Visualizing scandal growing in newspaper typography terms
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor for escalating scandal. Shows Amy's media savvy and urgency.

jokes.text: Feed the dog. Walk the dog. Shoot the dog. Bury the dog.
jokes.setup: Mike saying he can't work late because of his dog
jokes.punchline: Escalating from pet care to pet murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with dark humor. Shows Amy's ruthless priorities.

jokes.text: She's not funny. Of course I know that.
jokes.setup: Dan saying Selina was trying to be funny
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of boss's humor
jokes.explanation: Efficient, brutal honesty about Selina's comedy skills. Perfect Mike moment.

jokes.text: Your dick is hanging out of your pants.
jokes.setup: Telling Mike to tuck his shirt in
jokes.punchline: Escalating to crude anatomical criticism
jokes.explanation: Crude escalation showing Selina's morning mood. Functional but not clever.

jokes.text: Did the VEEP kill the last remaining snow leopard? Did she firebomb a hospice?
jokes.setup: Asking what colossal fuckup they're dealing with
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd scenarios of VP wrongdoing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of hypothetical scandals. Shows Jonah's dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: Did the VEEP kill the last remaining snow leopard? Did she firebomb a hospice?
jokes.setup: Jonah asking what colossal fuckup they're dealing with
jokes.punchline: His increasingly absurd suggestions of what she might have done
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how bad they expect things to be

jokes.text: What you got cooking? Dickwad pie?
jokes.setup: Greeting someone with 'what you got cooking?'
jokes.punchline: Rhyming insult following cooking theme
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyming insult. Crude but follows the setup logically.

jokes.text: When a sexual harasser dies, we sign his wife's card. Okay? That's how Washington works.
jokes.setup: Gary saying 'a man is dead' about senator Reeves
jokes.punchline: Cynical explanation of political etiquette despite moral objections
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about political hypocrisy. Perfect encapsulation of Washington cynicism.

jokes.text: That didn't get me.
jokes.setup: Gary demonstrating how Selina would sign someone else's name
jokes.punchline: Jonah admitting he doesn't understand the gesture
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's social cluelessness but isn't particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Yeah, right after I leaked that story.
jokes.setup: Saying he resigned from Hallowes's office an hour ago
jokes.punchline: Revealing he sabotaged his boss before quitting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment showing his treacherous nature. Efficient revelation of his scheming.

jokes.text: all you've got left in the last 10 remaining years of your working life is a damp apartment, cold crab cakes, and an invisible fucking dog.
jokes.setup: Dan asking Mike to consider whether Dan will be above or below him in a year
jokes.punchline: Devastating assessment of Mike's bleak future
jokes.explanation: Brutal, specific, and perfectly crafted insult. The 'invisible dog' callback is devastating and memorable.

jokes.text: How sure are you on a scale of one to 10? Like one is the... Yeah, yeah, I can work out how the fucking scale will go, Gary.
jokes.setup: Gary starting to explain a simple rating scale
jokes.punchline: Amy cutting him off irritably
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's stress and Gary's tendency to over-explain.

jokes.text: If I were writing a memoir, which I'm not, but if I were, whew, this would be a big moment.
jokes.setup: Describing the escalating crisis
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on the dramatic nature of events
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor showing Mike's awareness of their dysfunction. Good character specificity.

jokes.text: If I were writing a memoir, which I'm not, but if I were, whew, this would be a big moment.
jokes.setup: Gary realizing the magnitude of the mistake
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on his own unimportant life
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke that shows Gary's self-awareness about his insignificance

jokes.text: I am putting out two fires in there, and I turn around to find out that you have set fire to the fucking fire truck.
jokes.setup: Amy discovering Gary's condolence card mistake
jokes.punchline: Extended firefighting metaphor for crisis management
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor perfectly capturing the escalating crisis. Amy at her best.

jokes.text: Fucktard.
jokes.setup: Amy apologizing to the mental health advocate after saying the R-word
jokes.punchline: Immediately using another offensive portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Shows Amy's stress causing more mistakes. Cringe escalation but relies on offensive language.

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh, is this gonna become like 'Moonlighting' where we fall in love and start fucking?
jokes.setup: Dan saying Amy's happiness isn't his top priority
jokes.punchline: Reference to TV show's romantic subplot
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference showing Amy's exasperation with their dynamic. Moderately effective.

jokes.text: Are you here to steal the incorrectly-signed card?
jokes.setup: Jonah seeing Gary arrive
jokes.punchline: His immediate understanding of the obvious mission
jokes.explanation: Shows how transparent their schemes are

jokes.text: I want Amy to go on a date with me tonight. Don't worry about it, okay? We're not gonna have sex, all right? Because she hates me.
jokes.setup: Jonah's price for returning the card
jokes.punchline: Admitting Amy hates him while asking for date
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah moment showing his delusion and self-awareness simultaneously.

jokes.text: And if all the other eights that I do want to have sex with see that I am eight-capable, then I'm going to be having sex with an eight very soon. It is intern season, Gary.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining why he wants to date Amy
jokes.punchline: His rating system strategy and intern season reference
jokes.explanation: Reveals Jonah's shallow, predatory dating strategy

jokes.text: And if all the other eights that I do want to have sex with see that I am eight-capable, then I'm going to be having sex with an eight very soon. It is intern season, Gary.
jokes.setup: Explaining his dating strategy with Amy
jokes.punchline: Reducing women to numbers and timing it with intern arrivals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment showing his objectification and calculated thinking. Cringey but character-accurate.

jokes.text: Do I look like a pimp to you? You look exactly like a pimp.
jokes.setup: Gary asking if he looks like a pimp
jokes.punchline: Jonah's immediate confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with Jonah's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Do I look like a pimp to you? You look exactly like a pimp.
jokes.setup: Gary asking if he looks like a pimp
jokes.punchline: Jonah's immediate, confident affirmation
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with immediate, confident delivery. Visual comedy about Gary's appearance.

jokes.text: You look exactly like a pimp.
jokes.setup: Gary asking if he looks like a pimp
jokes.punchline: Jonah's immediate confirmation
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of the pimping metaphor

jokes.text: For me, that was 12 years of marriage.
jokes.setup: Jonah wanting a date with no sex
jokes.punchline: Mike comparing it to his sexless marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, efficient dark humor revealing Mike's personal life. Perfect timing and delivery.

jokes.text: Yeah, you could be legally electrocuted for doing that, just so you know.
jokes.setup: Discussing forging the President's signature as a capital offense
jokes.punchline: Specific, outdated execution method reference
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about consequences, but not exceptional. Shows Mike's fatalistic attitude.

jokes.text: Foreplay, direct sunlight.
jokes.setup: Asked if there's anything he can't do
jokes.punchline: Two very different personal limitations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, economical character revelation. The contrast between intimate and mundane is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, well, I'm fluent in bastard. Okay? It's one of my languages.
jokes.setup: Amy calling Dan the biggest bastard in DC
jokes.punchline: Selina claiming fluency in 'bastard' as a language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's self-awareness about managing difficult people.

jokes.text: I'm the user, and he's the usee.
jokes.setup: Defending her hiring of Dan
jokes.punchline: Made-up word to describe their relationship dynamic
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay showing Selina's delusion about controlling Dan. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Shrimp Linguini and a porno. Oh, hey. I'm just kidding. It's chicken Linguini.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing his planned date with Amy
jokes.punchline: Correcting the pasta type but not the porno part
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's priorities and social cluelessness. Misdirection works moderately well.

jokes.text: This is how they solved the Cuban missile crisis. This is my Cuba.
jokes.setup: Selina defending her political compromise
jokes.punchline: Comparing minor political issue to major historical crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina grandiosity - comparing trivial VP problems to world-changing events

jokes.text: This is my Cuba.
jokes.setup: Comparing her political maneuvering to Cuban Missile Crisis
jokes.punchline: Grandiose comparison of minor policy to major historical crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina delusion - comparing minor political maneuvering to world-changing crisis.

jokes.text: Amy, I need a shit.
jokes.setup: Amy continuing to warn about Dan being a shit
jokes.punchline: Selina saying she needs exactly what Amy's warning her about
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Selina deliberately wanting what Amy sees as a problem.

jokes.text: You got a brand-new desk in the corner. Follow me, I've got a beautiful spot for your adoring photo of girlfriend Carol Hallowes. Actually, she's not my girlfriend anymore. I just broke up with her about 15 minutes ago via text. Well, cold and rather nerdy. Actually, it was an app.
jokes.setup: Dan being given a desk and discussion of girlfriend photo
jokes.punchline: Revealing breakup via increasingly impersonal methods
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dan's callousness and modern dating dysfunction. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: Touch me and you lose a finger. And a ball.
jokes.setup: Implied threat of physical contact
jokes.punchline: Escalating threat with specific anatomical consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue moment - economical threat showing her no-nonsense attitude. Good escalation structure.

tmdb_overview: Vice President Selina Meyer and her staff - chief of staff Amy, spokesperson Mike, right-hand man Gary, and executive assistant Sue - are still finding their footing in Washington D.C. Selina decides to make the Clean Jobs Commission a pet project, but a series of missteps requires her staff to work damage control.
insight: Pilot establishes Veep's cringe engine with 89 jokes in 38 minutes, mostly escalation-driven character chaos.
